rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Banned words — grep zero-tolerance.
Ritual / practice (as noun) / ceremony — hard ban
rules_evaluated.evidence: 67:"A handful of manual interventions is the practice."
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Line 22 is the M4 earning sentence; line 67 is a second M4 body use before fluency opens at M5. Replace with plain operational description: "A handful of corrections is normal; past ten you've become the execution path."

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No stale markers, no "fixed" retrospectives, no session biography.
Register match.
Rough-cut drafts for articles.
Credit the original when paraphrasing a named technique.
Always "you", never "the student"; teacher framing stays out of body.
Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.
Over-hedge detector.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 67:"Nudging by hand is fine if you're watching."
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: "Fine if" is reassurance-shaped — implies the author thought it needed defending. Recast as a direct observation: "Nudge when you're watching. Answer a question, correct a path, push back on drift."

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verbatim-frame-cite in reference artefact.
Cap practitioner attribution at one student-side mention per module.
Punchy framing keeps its punch; carve-out lives downstream.
Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + project-level strategy docs, NOT in student-facing curriculum body.
Watch out for planting jargon too early.
Audit the closer when trimming a body-prose forcing function.
No combative verbs about the agent in body prose.
Aspirational destination, never corrective gap.
Earn every technical term on first use.
Business-audience word ban (Bootstrap default).
Memory vs context — use deliberately.
No overclaim of student verification or coverage
Golden path only in the body.
Trust Claude's defaults for structure.
Q1/Q2/Q3 retro interview is banned at every module.
Don't smuggle another module's voice.
Don't ask the student to manually diff or spot-check Claude's output as error-catching.
Simulation personas never alone.
No "What research says" callout boxes
Student moves stay in conversation with the agent.
Specify which CLAUDE.md layer — every time.
No em-dashes. Simple sentences.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 44:"tell you the short SHA — keep it noted"; 69:"dark-humour nudge — phrased as encouragement, lands as a taunt"; 87:"**Read — [Clean Code Is Steering...]"; 89:"**Watch — Laura Entis on Kieran Klaassen"; 91:"**Read (longer) — Kieran Klaassen"
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Five body em-dashes survive the auto-fix pass. Replace with comma or period splits: line 44 → "...short SHA. Keep it noted."; line 69 → "...dark-humour nudge, phrased as encouragement, lands as a taunt:"; pre-read callouts → use colon or comma as separator instead of em-dash.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Section headers use command verbs when the section is about doing something.
Inline links use the target's title, never its filename.
Disambiguate shared-word collisions with Claude Code primitives on first student-authored use.
LLM-vs-agent-vs-Claude vocabulary discipline.
No time-of-day anchors in body prose.
Reflection beats stay invisible in the flow.
No meta-ask when the student has already decided.
.md is the default file type for student artifacts across the curriculum.
Cut NVA: true but not moving the student forward.
No narrating the student's past or present state
rules_evaluated.evidence: 67:"Past ten and you've become the agent; call it and read what's there."
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Narrates the student's current state. Sutherland-flip: "Past ten nudges, the session is the agent's run. Call it, read what's there."
